title: palindrome-ignore-case-spaces
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a string is a palindrome, ignoring case and spaces. Returns True for the empty string and single characters.

title: palindrome-ignore-punctuation
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a string is a palindrome considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring case, so spaces and punctuation are skipped. Returns True for empty or all-punctuation strings.

title: palindrome-permutation
text: Write a Python function that checks whether the letters of a string can be rearranged to form a palindrome, ignoring case, spaces and non-letters. At most one letter may have an odd count; the empty string returns True.

title: longest-palindromic-substring
text: Write a Python function that returns the longest palindromic substring of a string using expand-around-center, returning the first one found on ties. Handles the empty string (returns an empty string) and single characters.

title: anagram-ignore-case-spaces
text: Write a Python function that checks whether two strings are anagrams of each other, ignoring case, spaces and punctuation. Two empty strings count as anagrams.
code:
def is_anagram(s1, s2):
    a = sorted(ch.lower() for ch in s1 if ch.isalnum())
    b = sorted(ch.lower() for ch in s2 if ch.isalnum())
    return a == b

title: group-anagrams
text: Write a Python function that groups a list of words into lists of anagrams using a case-insensitive sorted-letters key, preserving the order words first appear. Returns an empty list for an empty input list and handles a single word.

title: count-vowels-case-insensitive
text: Write a Python function that counts the vowels (a, e, i, o, u) in a string, case-insensitive so uppercase vowels are counted too. Returns 0 for the empty string or a string with no vowels.
code:
def count_vowels(s):
    return sum(1 for ch in s.lower() if ch in "aeiou")

title: count-consonants-case-insensitive
text: Write a Python function that counts the consonants in a string, case-insensitive, counting only alphabetic characters that are not vowels (digits, spaces and punctuation are ignored). Returns 0 for the empty string.
code:
def count_consonants(s):
    return sum(1 for ch in s.lower() if ch.isalpha() and ch not in "aeiou")

title: char-frequency-dict
text: Write a Python function that returns a dictionary mapping each character of a string to how many times it occurs, case-sensitive and including spaces and punctuation. Returns an empty dict for the empty string.

title: letter-frequency-case-insensitive
text: Write a Python function that returns a dictionary of letter frequencies in a string, case-insensitive, counting only alphabetic characters and ignoring digits, spaces and punctuation. Returns an empty dict when there are no letters.

title: reverse-string-loop
text: Write a Python function that reverses a string using a loop, without using slicing or reversed(). Handles the empty string and single characters.

title: reverse-word-order
text: Write a Python function that reverses the order of the words in a sentence, collapsing runs of extra whitespace to single spaces. Returns an empty string for empty or all-space input and handles single-word input.
code:
def reverse_words(s):
    return " ".join(s.split()[::-1])

title: reverse-each-word
text: Write a Python function that reverses the letters of each word in a sentence while keeping the words in their original order. Handles the empty string and single words.
code:
def reverse_each_word(s):
    return " ".join(word[::-1] for word in s.split(" "))

title: reverse-vowels-only
text: Write a Python function that reverses only the vowels of a string (both uppercase and lowercase), leaving every other character in place. Handles the empty string and strings with no vowels.

title: title-case-words
text: Write a Python function that converts a sentence to title case: the first letter of every word uppercase and the rest lowercase. Handles the empty string, single-letter words, and words that are already all caps.

title: capitalize-sentence-starts
text: Write a Python function that capitalizes the first letter of each sentence in a text, where sentences end with '.', '!' or '?'. All other characters are left unchanged; handles the empty string.

title: longest-word
text: Write a Python function that returns the longest word in a sentence, stripping punctuation so trailing commas or exclamation marks do not count toward word length. Returns the first word on ties and an empty string for empty input.

title: string-compression
text: Write a Python function that compresses a string by replacing runs of repeated characters with the character followed by its count (e.g. 'aabcccccaaa' becomes 'a2b1c5a3'), returning the original string if the compressed version is not strictly shorter. Handles the empty string.

title: first-non-repeating-char
text: Write a Python function that returns the first character in a string that appears exactly once, or None if every character repeats or the string is empty.

title: first-repeating-char
text: Write a Python function that returns the first character whose second occurrence appears earliest in a string, or None if no character repeats or the string is empty.

title: swap-case-manual
text: Write a Python function that swaps the case of every letter in a string (uppercase becomes lowercase and vice versa) without using str.swapcase(), leaving digits, spaces and punctuation unchanged. Handles the empty string.

title: count-substring-non-overlapping
text: Write a Python function that counts the non-overlapping occurrences of a substring inside a string. Returns 0 when the substring is empty or never occurs.

title: count-substring-overlapping
text: Write a Python function that counts occurrences of a substring in a string including overlapping matches (e.g. 'aa' occurs 3 times in 'aaaa'). Returns 0 for an empty substring or empty text.

title: find-substring-index-manual
text: Write a Python function that returns the index of the first occurrence of a substring in a string without using str.find or str.index, returning -1 if it is absent, 0 for an empty needle, and -1 when the needle is longer than the text.

title: remove-duplicate-chars-preserve-order
text: Write a Python function that removes duplicate characters from a string, keeping only the first occurrence of each character and preserving order (case-sensitive, so 'a' and 'A' are different). Handles the empty string and strings with no duplicates.

title: remove-duplicate-words-preserve-order
text: Write a Python function that removes duplicate words from a sentence, comparing words case-insensitively while keeping the first occurrence's casing and the original order. Handles the empty string.

title: acronym-from-phrase
text: Write a Python function that builds an uppercase acronym from the first letter of each word in a phrase, tolerating extra spaces between words. Returns an empty string for empty input.
code:
def make_acronym(phrase):
    return "".join(word[0].upper() for word in phrase.split())

title: camel-to-snake
text: Write a Python function that converts a camelCase or PascalCase identifier to snake_case, without adding a leading underscore for an initial capital letter. Leaves already-snake_case names and the empty string unchanged.

title: snake-to-camel
text: Write a Python function that converts a snake_case identifier to camelCase, handling consecutive underscores, single words without any underscore, and the empty string.

title: run-length-encode
text: Write a Python function that run-length encodes a string as character+count pairs (e.g. 'aaabccc' becomes 'a3b1c3'), including counts of 1 and multi-digit counts. Returns an empty string for empty input.

title: run-length-decode
text: Write a Python function that decodes a run-length encoded string of character+count pairs (e.g. 'a3b1c3' becomes 'aaabccc'), handling multi-digit counts and a bare character with no digits after it. Returns an empty string for empty input.

title: caesar-cipher-encrypt
text: Write a Python function that encrypts text with a Caesar cipher given a shift, preserving letter case, wrapping around the alphabet, leaving spaces, digits and punctuation unchanged, and handling shifts larger than 26 or negative shifts.

title: caesar-cipher-decrypt
text: Write a Python function that decrypts Caesar-cipher text by shifting letters back by the given shift, preserving case, wrapping around the alphabet, and leaving spaces, digits and punctuation unchanged. Handles the empty string.

title: extract-digits
text: Write a Python function that extracts only the digit characters from a string, in their original order, returned as a string. Returns an empty string when the input is empty or contains no digits.
code:
def extract_digits(s):
    return "".join(ch for ch in s if ch.isdigit())

title: sum-digits-in-string
text: Write a Python function that sums all the digit characters found in a string (e.g. 'a1b2c3' gives 6). Returns 0 for the empty string or a string without digits.
code:
def sum_digits(s):
    return sum(int(ch) for ch in s if ch.isdigit())

title: balanced-brackets
text: Write a Python function that checks whether the brackets (), [], {} in a string are balanced and properly nested using a stack, ignoring all non-bracket characters. The empty string is balanced; unclosed openers or mismatched pairs are not.

title: word-count
text: Write a Python function that counts the words in a string separated by whitespace, treating runs of multiple spaces as a single separator. Returns 0 for an empty or all-whitespace string.
code:
def word_count(s):
    return len(s.split())

title: word-frequency
text: Write a Python function that returns a dictionary of word frequencies in a text, case-insensitive, stripping surrounding punctuation such as periods, commas and question marks from each word. Returns an empty dict for empty input.

title: most-common-word
text: Write a Python function that returns the most frequent word in a text, case-insensitive, stripping surrounding punctuation, returning the earliest-seen word on ties and an empty string for empty input.

title: truncate-with-ellipsis
text: Write a Python function that truncates a string to a maximum length, appending '...' so the total result is at most the limit. Returns the string unchanged if it already fits, and falls back to plain slicing when the limit is 3 or less.

title: rotate-string-left
text: Write a Python function that rotates a string left by n positions, wrapping with modulo when n is larger than the string length, and handling n equal to 0 and the empty string.

title: is-rotation-of
text: Write a Python function that checks whether one string is a rotation of another using the doubled-string trick, returning False when the lengths differ and True for two empty strings.

title: isogram-check
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a string is an isogram (no letter repeats), case-insensitive and ignoring spaces, hyphens and digits. The empty string returns True.

title: pangram-check
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a string is a pangram containing every letter of the English alphabet at least once, case-insensitive. The empty string returns False.

title: missing-pangram-letters
text: Write a Python function that returns the letters of the English alphabet missing from a string, in alphabetical order, case-insensitive. Returns an empty string for a pangram and the whole alphabet for the empty string.

title: roman-to-int
text: Write a Python function that converts a Roman numeral string to an integer, handling subtractive pairs like IV, IX, XL, XC, CD and CM, and accepting lowercase input.

title: int-to-roman
text: Write a Python function that converts an integer from 1 to 3999 into its Roman numeral string, using subtractive notations like IV, IX, XL, XC, CD and CM.

title: string-to-int-manual
text: Write a Python function that converts a numeric string to an integer without using int(), handling leading and trailing whitespace, an optional '+' or '-' sign, and returning 0 for an empty string.

title: zigzag-conversion
text: Write a Python function that writes a string in a zigzag pattern on a given number of rows and reads it back row by row. Returns the string unchanged when the number of rows is 1 or at least the string length.

title: longest-common-prefix
text: Write a Python function that returns the longest common prefix of a list of strings, returning an empty string for an empty list, when any string is empty, or when there is no common prefix.

title: remove-vowels
text: Write a Python function that removes all vowels (both uppercase and lowercase) from a string, keeping consonants, digits, spaces and punctuation. Handles the empty string and strings with no vowels.
code:
def remove_vowels(s):
    return "".join(ch for ch in s if ch.lower() not in "aeiou")

title: hamming-distance
text: Write a Python function that computes the Hamming distance between two equal-length strings (the number of positions at which they differ), raising ValueError for unequal lengths and returning 0 for two empty strings.

title: count-upper-lower
text: Write a Python function that returns a tuple (uppercase_count, lowercase_count) for a string, ignoring digits, spaces and punctuation. Returns (0, 0) for the empty string.

title: most-frequent-char
text: Write a Python function that returns the most frequent letter in a string, case-insensitive and ignoring digits, spaces and punctuation, breaking ties by first occurrence in the string. Returns an empty string when there are no letters.

title: repeated-substring-pattern
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a string can be built by repeating one of its substrings two or more times (e.g. 'abab' is 'ab' repeated). Empty and single-character strings return False.

title: merge-two-sorted-lists
text: Write a Python function that merges two already-sorted lists into one sorted list without using sort() or sorted(), using two pointers. Handles empty lists on either side and duplicate values across both lists.

title: kth-smallest-distinct
text: Write a Python function that returns the k-th smallest DISTINCT value in a list (duplicates count once). Returns None when the list has fewer than k distinct values or k is not positive; handles negatives and single-element lists.

title: kth-smallest-positional
text: Write a Python function that returns the k-th smallest element of a list counting duplicates (the element at position k in sorted order). Returns None for an empty list, k less than 1, or k larger than the list length.

title: kth-largest-distinct
text: Write a Python function that returns the k-th largest DISTINCT value in a list, so duplicates count only once. Returns None when there are fewer than k distinct values; works with negatives and a single element.

title: kth-largest-positional
text: Write a Python function that returns the k-th largest element of a list counting duplicates (position k in descending sorted order). Returns None for an empty list or out-of-range k; duplicates can occupy several positions.

title: third-smallest-distinct
text: Write a Python function that returns the third smallest distinct number in a list, ignoring duplicate values. Returns None if the list has fewer than three distinct numbers; handles negatives.

title: remove-duplicates-preserve-order
text: Write a Python function that removes duplicate elements from a list while preserving the original order of first occurrences. Handles an empty list, a single element, and lists where every element is the same.

title: rotate-list-right
text: Write a Python function that rotates a list to the right by k positions and returns a new list. Handles an empty list, a single element, and k larger than the list length (wraps around with modulo).

title: two-sum-indices
text: Write a Python function that finds the indices of two numbers in a list that add up to a target, using a one-pass dictionary. Handles duplicate values that pair with themselves and returns None when no pair exists.

title: max-without-builtin
text: Write a Python function that finds the maximum value in a list without using the built-in max() function. Returns None for an empty list; handles all-negative lists and a single element.

title: min-without-builtin
text: Write a Python function that finds the minimum value in a list without using the built-in min() function. Returns None for an empty list; handles negatives and a single element.

title: second-largest-distinct
text: Write a Python function that returns the second largest distinct number in a list in a single pass without sorting. Returns None when all elements are equal or the list has fewer than two distinct values; handles negatives.

title: second-smallest-distinct
text: Write a Python function that returns the second smallest distinct number in a list in a single pass without sorting. Returns None when there are fewer than two distinct values; duplicates of the minimum do not count as second smallest.

title: list-intersection-preserve-order
text: Write a Python function that returns the intersection of two lists: elements present in both, in the order they first appear in the first list, with no duplicates in the output. Handles empty lists and repeated elements.

title: list-union-preserve-order
text: Write a Python function that returns the union of two lists preserving first-appearance order: all unique elements of the first list followed by new unique elements of the second. Handles empty lists and duplicates within each list.

title: list-difference-preserve-order
text: Write a Python function that returns the elements of the first list that do not appear in the second list, preserving order and keeping repeated occurrences. Handles empty lists and the case where everything is removed.

title: chunk-list-fixed-size
text: Write a Python function that splits a list into consecutive chunks of a given size, where the last chunk may be shorter. Handles an empty list, a chunk size larger than the list, and returns an empty list for non-positive sizes.

title: flatten-one-level
text: Write a Python function that flattens a list by one level: sublist elements are spliced in, non-list items are kept as-is. Handles an empty outer list, empty sublists, and mixed lists containing both scalars and sublists.

title: flatten-deep-nested
text: Write a Python function that fully flattens an arbitrarily nested list of lists into a single flat list using recursion. Handles empty lists, empty nested lists, and nesting of any depth.

title: running-sum-prefix
text: Write a Python function that returns the running sum (prefix sums) of a list, where each output element is the sum of all inputs up to that index. Handles an empty list, a single element, and negative numbers.
code:
def running_sum(nums):
    result = []
    total = 0
    for x in nums:
        total += x
        result.append(total)
    return result

title: product-except-self
text: Write a Python function that returns a list where each element is the product of all other elements, without using division. Handles zeros in the input, negatives, a single element (returns [1]), and an empty list.
code:
def product_except_self(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    result = [1] * n
    left = 1
    for i in range(n):
        result[i] = left
        left *= nums[i]
    right = 1
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        result[i] *= right
        right *= nums[i]
    return result

title: move-zeros-to-end
text: Write a Python function that moves all zeros in a list to the end while keeping the relative order of the non-zero elements. Handles an all-zero list, a list with no zeros, and an empty list.

title: find-missing-number
text: Write a Python function that finds the single missing number in a list containing distinct integers from 1 to n with exactly one missing, using the expected-sum formula. Handles the missing number being 1, n, or in the middle.

title: majority-element
text: Write a Python function that finds the majority element of a list (the value appearing more than half the time) using the Boyer-Moore vote with a verification pass. Returns None when no majority exists or the list is empty.

title: pairs-summing-to-target
text: Write a Python function that finds all unique pairs of values in a list that sum to a target, returning each pair once as a sorted tuple in a sorted list. Handles duplicates (a value pairing with its own copy), negatives, and no matches.

title: max-sum-sliding-window
text: Write a Python function that finds the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of exactly size k using a sliding window. Returns None when k is larger than the list or not positive; handles all-negative lists.

title: longest-increasing-run
text: Write a Python function that returns the length of the longest strictly increasing contiguous run in a list. Returns 0 for an empty list and 1 for a single element or a fully non-increasing list; equal neighbors break the run.

title: interleave-two-lists
text: Write a Python function that interleaves two lists element by element, appending the leftover tail when the lists have different lengths. Handles one or both lists being empty.

title: split-into-evens-odds
text: Write a Python function that splits a list of integers into two lists, evens and odds, preserving the original order. Handles zero (even), negative numbers, and an empty input; returns the pair (evens, odds).

title: binary-search-index
text: Write a Python function that performs binary search on a sorted list and returns an index of the target, or -1 if it is absent. Handles an empty list, a single element, and targets outside the range.

title: binary-search-first-occurrence
text: Write a Python function that returns the index of the FIRST occurrence of a target in a sorted list with duplicates using binary search, or -1 if absent. Handles an empty list and a list that is entirely the target.

title: binary-search-last-occurrence
text: Write a Python function that returns the index of the LAST occurrence of a target in a sorted list with duplicates using binary search, or -1 if absent. Handles an empty list and single-element lists.

title: sort-words-by-length-then-alpha
text: Write a Python function that sorts a list of strings by length, breaking ties alphabetically, using a named key function. Handles an empty list, equal-length words, and single-element lists.

title: count-inversions
text: Write a Python function that counts inversions in a list: pairs of indices i < j where nums[i] > nums[j], using a simple double loop. Handles an already-sorted list (0), a reverse-sorted list, duplicates, and an empty list.

title: dedupe-adjacent-elements
text: Write a Python function that collapses consecutive duplicate elements in a list, keeping one copy of each run (like uniq). Non-adjacent duplicates are kept; handles empty lists and lists where all elements are identical.

title: reverse-list-manual
text: Write a Python function that reverses a list and returns a new list without using reversed(), .reverse(), or slice tricks. Handles an empty list and a single element.

title: is-sorted-non-decreasing
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a list is sorted in non-decreasing order (equal neighbors allowed). Returns True for an empty list and a single element; handles negatives.

title: count-occurrences-of-value
text: Write a Python function that counts how many times a given value appears in a list without using the built-in .count() method. Handles an empty list and a value that never appears (returns 0).

title: find-all-indices-of-value
text: Write a Python function that returns a list of all indices where a given value occurs in a list. Returns an empty list when the value is absent or the input is empty.

title: max-subarray-sum-kadane
text: Write a Python function that finds the maximum sum of any contiguous non-empty subarray using Kadane's algorithm. Handles all-negative lists (returns the largest single element), a single element, and returns 0 for an empty list.

title: cumulative-product
text: Write a Python function that returns the cumulative (running) product of a list, where each output element is the product of all inputs up to that index. Handles an empty list, zeros, and negative numbers.
code:
def cumulative_product(nums):
    result = []
    product = 1
    for x in nums:
        product *= x
        result.append(product)
    return result

title: adjacent-pairs
text: Write a Python function that returns a list of tuples of each pair of adjacent elements in a list. Returns an empty list when the input has fewer than two elements.

title: most-frequent-element
text: Write a Python function that returns the most frequent element in a list, breaking ties by whichever element appears first. Returns None for an empty list; works for strings and numbers.

title: remove-all-occurrences
text: Write a Python function that removes every occurrence of a given value from a list, returning a new list with the original order preserved. Handles the value being absent, present multiple times, or the list being empty.

title: longest-consecutive-sequence
text: Write a Python function that finds the length of the longest run of consecutive integers (e.g. 1,2,3,4) present anywhere in an unsorted list, ignoring duplicates. Returns 0 for an empty list; handles negatives.

title: equilibrium-index
text: Write a Python function that finds the first equilibrium index of a list: the index where the sum of elements to the left equals the sum to the right. Returns -1 when none exists; handles negatives, a single element (index 0), and an empty list.

title: leaders-in-array
text: Write a Python function that returns all leaders in a list: elements strictly greater than every element to their right (the last element is always a leader), in original order. Handles duplicates, an empty list, and a single element.

title: merge-overlapping-intervals
text: Write a Python function that merges overlapping intervals in a list of [start, end] pairs and returns them sorted by start. Handles touching intervals (end == next start), unsorted input, fully contained intervals, and an empty list.

title: single-number-xor
text: Write a Python function that finds the element appearing exactly once in a list where every other element appears exactly twice, using XOR in one pass. Handles negatives, zero, and a single-element list.

title: sorted-squares
text: Write a Python function that takes a list of integers (possibly negative) and returns a list of their squares in ascending sorted order. Handles negatives whose squares outrank positives, an empty list, and a single element.

title: find-duplicate-values
text: Write a Python function that returns the values that appear more than once in a list, each reported once, in order of their first appearance. Returns an empty list when all elements are unique or the list is empty.

title: is-rotation-of-list
text: Write a Python function that checks whether one list is a rotation of another (same elements shifted circularly), by scanning the first list doubled. Handles different lengths (False), empty lists (True), and identical lists.

title: min-difference-pair
text: Write a Python function that finds the minimum absolute difference between any two elements of a list by sorting and scanning adjacent values. Returns None when the list has fewer than two elements; handles duplicates (difference 0) and negatives.

title: average-of-list
text: Write a Python function that computes the average (mean) of a list of numbers as a float. Returns None for an empty list; handles negatives and a single element.

title: factorial-iterative
text: Write a Python function that computes the factorial of a non-negative integer n using a loop (iteratively). It must return 1 for n == 0 and n == 1, and raise ValueError for negative input.

title: factorial-recursive
text: Write a recursive Python function that computes the factorial of a non-negative integer n, with n == 0 as the base case returning 1. Raise ValueError for negative input.

title: fibonacci-nth
text: Write a Python function that returns the nth Fibonacci number, where F(0) = 0 and F(1) = 1. It must handle n == 0 and n == 1 correctly and raise ValueError for negative n.

title: fibonacci-first-n-list
text: Write a Python function that returns a list of the first n Fibonacci numbers starting from 0 (i.e. 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, ...). It must return an empty list for n == 0 and [0] for n == 1.
code:
def fibonacci_list(n):
    seq = []
    a, b = 0, 1
    for _ in range(n):
        seq.append(a)
        a, b = b, a + b
    return seq

title: prime-check
text: Write a Python function that checks whether an integer is a prime number, returning True or False. It must return False for numbers less than 2 (including 0, 1, and negatives), True for 2, and use trial division up to the square root for efficiency.

title: primes-up-to-n-sieve
text: Write a Python function that returns a list of all prime numbers up to and including n, using the Sieve of Eratosthenes. It must return an empty list when n is less than 2.

title: gcd-euclidean
text: Write a Python function that computes the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two integers using the Euclidean algorithm, without using math.gcd. It must handle zero (gcd(0, x) == x) and negative inputs (result is always non-negative).

title: lcm-two-numbers
text: Write a Python function that computes the least common multiple (LCM) of two integers using the GCD, without importing math. It must return 0 when either input is 0 and give a non-negative result for negative inputs.

title: digit-sum
text: Write a Python function that returns the sum of the digits of an integer using arithmetic (no string conversion). It must return 0 for input 0 and handle negative numbers by summing the digits of the absolute value.
code:
def digit_sum(n):
    n = abs(n)
    total = 0
    while n > 0:
        total += n % 10
        n //= 10
    return total

title: digit-product
text: Write a Python function that returns the product of the digits of an integer. It must return 0 for input 0, handle negative numbers via absolute value, and correctly return 0 when any digit is 0.

title: reverse-integer-digits
text: Write a Python function that reverses the digits of an integer using arithmetic (no string slicing). Negative numbers keep their sign (-450 becomes -54), trailing zeros are dropped, and 0 returns 0.
code:
def reverse_number(n):
    sign = -1 if n < 0 else 1
    n = abs(n)
    result = 0
    while n > 0:
        result = result * 10 + n % 10
        n //= 10
    return sign * result

title: palindromic-number
text: Write a Python function that checks whether an integer is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards) using arithmetic digit reversal, not strings. Negative numbers are never palindromes, single-digit numbers and 0 are palindromes.

title: armstrong-number
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a number is an Armstrong (narcissistic) number: the sum of its digits each raised to the power of the digit count equals the number itself. Single-digit numbers and 0 are Armstrong numbers; negatives are not.

title: perfect-number
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a number is a perfect number (equal to the sum of its proper divisors, e.g. 6 = 1+2+3). It must return False for numbers below 2 and use divisor pairs up to the square root so large inputs like 496 are fast.

title: power-without-operator
text: Write a Python function that computes base raised to an integer exponent without using the ** operator or pow(). It must return 1 for exponent 0, handle negative exponents by returning the reciprocal, and work with negative bases.

title: integer-square-root
text: Write a Python function that returns the integer square root (floor of the square root) of a non-negative integer without using math.sqrt, using binary search. It must handle 0 and 1, non-perfect squares (floor), and raise ValueError for negative input.

title: decimal-to-binary
text: Write a Python function that converts a decimal integer to its binary representation as a string, without using bin(). It must return "0" for input 0 and prefix a minus sign for negative numbers.

title: binary-to-decimal
text: Write a Python function that converts a binary string to its decimal integer value without using int(s, 2). It must handle "0", a leading minus sign for negative values, and surrounding whitespace.

title: decimal-to-hex
text: Write a Python function that converts a decimal integer to its lowercase hexadecimal string without using hex() or format(). It must return "0" for input 0 and prefix a minus sign for negative numbers.
code:
def to_hex(n):
    if n == 0:
        return "0"
    digits = "0123456789abcdef"
    sign = "-" if n < 0 else ""
    n = abs(n)
    out = ""
    while n > 0:
        out = digits[n % 16] + out
        n //= 16
    return sign + out

title: hex-to-decimal
text: Write a Python function that converts a hexadecimal string to its decimal integer value without using int(s, 16). It must accept upper or lower case digits, an optional "0x" prefix, a leading minus sign, and surrounding whitespace.

title: decimal-to-base-n
text: Write a Python function that converts a decimal integer to a string in any base from 2 to 16 (digits above 9 use uppercase A-F). It must return "0" for input 0, handle negative numbers with a minus sign, and raise ValueError for an unsupported base.

title: percentage-of-number
text: Write a Python function that calculates what percent% of a whole is (e.g. 25% of 200 is 50), returning the result rounded to 2 decimal places. It must handle 0 percent and fractional results.
code:
def percentage_of(percent, whole):
    return round(percent * whole / 100.0, 2)

title: discount-price-two-decimals
text: Write a Python function that calculates the final price after applying a percentage discount and returns it as a string formatted with exactly 2 decimal places (e.g. "75.00"). It must handle a 0% discount and non-integer prices.

title: price-with-tax-two-decimals
text: Write a Python function that calculates the total price after adding a percentage sales tax and returns it as a string formatted with exactly 2 decimal places. It must handle 0% tax and fractional tax rates like 7.5%.

title: percent-change
text: Write a Python function that computes the percentage change from an old value to a new value, rounded to 2 decimal places (positive for increase, negative for decrease). It must return 0.0 when values are equal and raise ValueError when the old value is 0.

title: compound-interest
text: Write a Python function that computes the final amount from compound interest given principal, annual rate percent, years, and compounding periods per year (default 1), rounded to 2 decimal places. It must handle 0 years and a 0% rate (returns the principal).

title: simple-interest
text: Write a Python function that computes simple interest (principal * rate * time / 100) given principal, annual rate percent, and years, rounded to 2 decimal places. It must handle zero principal and a 0% rate.

title: average-of-list
text: Write a Python function that returns the average (arithmetic mean) of a list of numbers without using the statistics module. It must return 0.0 for an empty list and handle single elements and negative numbers.

title: median-of-list
text: Write a Python function that returns the median of a list of numbers without using the statistics module. It must sort a copy of the input, average the two middle values for even-length lists, handle a single element and duplicates, and raise ValueError for an empty list.

title: mode-of-list
text: Write a Python function that returns the mode (most frequent value) of a list of numbers without using the statistics module or collections.Counter. On a tie it returns the smallest of the tied values; it handles single elements and negatives and raises ValueError for an empty list.

title: clamp-value
text: Write a Python function that clamps a number into the inclusive range [low, high]: values below low return low, values above high return high, values inside are returned unchanged. It must raise ValueError if low > high and handle values exactly at the boundaries.

title: celsius-to-fahrenheit
text: Write a Python function that converts a temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit using F = C * 9/5 + 32, rounded to 2 decimal places. It must handle 0, negative temperatures, and the -40 crossover point.
code:
def celsius_to_fahrenheit(c):
    return round(c * 9.0 / 5.0 + 32, 2)

title: fahrenheit-to-celsius
text: Write a Python function that converts a temperature from Fahrenheit to Celsius using C = (F - 32) * 5/9, rounded to 2 decimal places. It must handle the freezing point 32F, boiling point 212F, negatives, and the -40 crossover point.
code:
def fahrenheit_to_celsius(f):
    return round((f - 32) * 5.0 / 9.0, 2)

title: temperature-converter-general
text: Write a Python function that converts a temperature between Celsius, Fahrenheit, and Kelvin given the value plus source and target unit names, returning the result rounded to 2 decimal places. Unit names are case-insensitive and may be full words ("celsius") or single letters ("C"); converting a unit to itself returns the same value.

title: leap-year
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a year is a leap year using the full Gregorian rule: divisible by 4, except century years, which must be divisible by 400 (so 2000 is a leap year but 1900 and 2100 are not).
code:
def is_leap_year(year):
    return year % 4 == 0 and (year % 100 != 0 or year % 400 == 0)

title: collatz-steps
text: Write a Python function that counts how many Collatz steps (halve if even, 3n+1 if odd) it takes to reach 1 from a positive integer n. It must return 0 for n == 1 and raise ValueError for n < 1.

title: sum-of-multiples-3-5
text: Write a Python function that returns the sum of all multiples of 3 or 5 strictly below n (Project Euler problem 1 style), counting numbers divisible by both only once. It must return 0 for n == 0 or n == 1.

title: triangle-validity
text: Write a Python function that checks whether three side lengths can form a valid triangle using the triangle inequality (each pair of sides must sum to strictly more than the third). It must return False for zero or negative sides and for degenerate triangles like (1, 2, 3).

title: quadratic-roots
text: Write a Python function that solves the quadratic equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0 using the discriminant, returning a sorted list of real roots: two roots when the discriminant is positive, one when it is zero, and an empty list when it is negative. It must raise ValueError when a == 0.

title: round-to-nearest-step
text: Write a Python function that rounds a number to the nearest multiple of a given step (e.g. nearest 5 or nearest 25), with exact halves rounding away from zero. It must handle negative values, zero, and raise ValueError for a non-positive step.

title: prime-factorization
text: Write a Python function that returns the prime factorization of an integer as a list of prime factors with repetition in ascending order (12 -> [2, 2, 3]). It must return an empty list for numbers below 2 and handle primes themselves (13 -> [13]).

title: count-digits
text: Write a Python function that counts the number of digits in an integer using arithmetic (no string conversion). It must return 1 for input 0 and ignore the sign of negative numbers.

title: digital-root
text: Write a Python function that repeatedly sums the digits of an integer until a single digit remains (the digital root, e.g. 38 -> 11 -> 2). It must return 0 for input 0, handle negatives via absolute value, and return single-digit inputs unchanged.

title: divisors-of-n
text: Write a Python function that returns all positive divisors of a positive integer in ascending order, finding divisor pairs only up to the square root for efficiency. It must return [1] for input 1, not duplicate the square root of perfect squares, and return an empty list for inputs below 1.

title: nth-prime
text: Write a Python function that returns the nth prime number using 1-based counting, so the 1st prime is 2 and the 6th is 13. It must raise ValueError for n < 1 and test candidates by trial division up to the square root.

title: word-frequency-from-text
text: Write a Python function that builds a word-frequency dictionary from a text string, case-insensitively and with surrounding punctuation stripped from each word. Handles empty text and repeated words in different cases.

title: invert-dict-simple
text: Write a Python function that inverts a dictionary, swapping keys and values. Handles the empty dictionary; when two keys share a value, the later key wins.
code:
def invert_dict(d):
    return {v: k for k, v in d.items()}

title: invert-dict-group-duplicate-values
text: Write a Python function that inverts a dictionary where several keys may map to the same value, so each value maps to the list of all keys that had it. Handles empty input and single entries.

title: merge-two-dicts-sum-values
text: Write a Python function that merges two dictionaries of numbers; when a key appears in both, its values are added together. Handles empty dictionaries and negative values, and does not modify the inputs.

title: merge-list-of-dicts-sum-values
text: Write a Python function that merges a list of dictionaries of numbers into one dictionary, summing the values of keys that appear more than once. Handles an empty list, empty dictionaries in the list, and negative values.

title: group-words-by-first-letter
text: Write a Python function that groups a list of words into a dictionary keyed by lowercase first letter, preserving each word's original spelling and order. Handles an empty list, empty strings in the list, and mixed case.

title: group-anagrams
text: Write a Python function that groups a list of words into lists of anagrams of each other, case-insensitively, preserving first-seen order. Handles an empty list and single words.

title: top-k-keys-by-value
text: Write a Python function that returns the k keys with the largest values in a dictionary, sorted from largest value down. Handles k larger than the dictionary size, the empty dictionary, and ties (earlier-inserted key first).

title: dict-from-two-lists
text: Write a Python function that builds a dictionary from a list of keys and a list of values. Handles empty lists and lists of unequal length by pairing only up to the shorter one.
code:
def lists_to_dict(keys, values):
    return dict(zip(keys, values))

title: nested-dict-get-with-default
text: Write a Python function that safely fetches a value from a nested dictionary given a list of keys forming a path, returning a default when any key is missing or an intermediate value is not a dictionary. An empty path returns the dictionary itself.

title: count-items-by-category
text: Write a Python function that takes a list of (item, category) pairs and returns a dictionary counting how many items fall in each category. Handles an empty list and a single pair.

title: fifo-queue-with-list
text: Write a Python class implementing a FIFO queue backed by a list, with enqueue, dequeue, is_empty and size methods. Dequeue on an empty queue returns None instead of raising.

title: stack-with-list
text: Write a Python class implementing a stack (LIFO) backed by a list, with push, pop, peek and is_empty methods. Pop and peek on an empty stack return None instead of raising.

title: days-between-dates-manual
text: Write a Python function that computes the absolute number of days between two dates given as 'YYYY-MM-DD' strings, using a manual month-length table with correct leap-year handling (divisible by 4, except centuries unless divisible by 400). Handles identical dates and dates in either order.

title: validate-email-simple-rules
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a string looks like a valid email using simple rules: exactly one '@', non-empty local and domain parts, a '.' in the domain that is not at its start or end, and no spaces. Returns False for anything that breaks a rule.

title: matrix-transpose
text: Write a Python function that transposes a matrix given as a list of lists, so rows become columns. Handles the empty matrix, a single row, and a single element.

title: matrix-main-diagonal-sum
text: Write a Python function that returns the sum of the main diagonal of a square matrix (list of lists). Handles the empty matrix (sum 0) and a 1x1 matrix.
code:
def diagonal_sum(matrix):
    return sum(matrix[i][i] for i in range(len(matrix)))

title: matrix-both-diagonals-sum
text: Write a Python function that sums both diagonals of a square matrix, counting the center element only once when the size is odd. Handles the empty matrix and a 1x1 matrix.

title: sort-tuples-by-second-element
text: Write a Python function that sorts a list of tuples by their second element in ascending order, keeping the original order for ties (stable sort). Handles an empty list and a single tuple, and does not modify the input.
code:
def sort_by_second(pairs):
    return sorted(pairs, key=lambda t: t[1])

title: char-frequency-ignore-case-spaces
text: Write a Python function that counts how often each character occurs in a string, case-insensitively and ignoring spaces. Handles the empty string and strings of only spaces; punctuation is counted like any other character.

title: most-frequent-word
text: Write a Python function that returns the most frequent word in a text string, case-insensitively with punctuation stripped; on a tie the word that appeared first wins. Returns None for empty text.

title: filter-dict-by-min-value
text: Write a Python function that returns a new dictionary keeping only the entries whose value is greater than or equal to a threshold. Handles the empty dictionary, negative values, and the case where nothing passes.
code:
def filter_by_min_value(d, minimum):
    return {k: v for k, v in d.items() if v >= minimum}

title: key-with-max-value
text: Write a Python function that returns the key with the largest value in a dictionary; on a tie the earlier-inserted key wins, and the empty dictionary returns None. Handles negative values.

title: count-value-occurrences-in-dict
text: Write a Python function that counts how many times each value appears in a dictionary, returning a value-to-count dictionary. Handles the empty dictionary and non-numeric values such as strings.

title: flatten-nested-dict-dotted-keys
text: Write a Python function that flattens a nested dictionary into a single-level dictionary whose keys are the paths joined with dots (e.g. 'a.b.c'). Handles the empty dictionary, values that are empty dictionaries (kept as-is), and arbitrary nesting depth.

title: is-leap-year
text: Write a Python function that checks whether a year is a leap year: divisible by 4, except century years which must be divisible by 400. Handles the classic traps 1900 (not leap) and 2000 (leap).
code:
def is_leap_year(year):
    return year % 4 == 0 and (year % 100 != 0 or year % 400 == 0)

title: validate-date-string
text: Write a Python function that validates a date string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format, checking the shape, that the month is 1-12, and that the day fits the month including February 29 only in leap years. Returns False for malformed strings instead of raising.

title: count-unique-words
text: Write a Python function that counts the number of distinct words in a text string, case-insensitively and with punctuation stripped. Handles empty text (returns 0) and repeated words in different cases.

title: sort-dict-by-value
text: Write a Python function that returns a dictionary's items as a list of (key, value) tuples sorted by value, descending by default with an option for ascending. Handles the empty dictionary and a single entry.
code:
def sort_dict_by_value(d, descending=True):
    return sorted(d.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1], reverse=descending)

title: group-records-by-field
text: Write a Python function that groups a list of dictionaries (records) by the value of a given field, returning a dict mapping each field value to the list of records that have it. Handles an empty list and records missing the field (grouped under None).

title: pairs-to-dict-of-lists
text: Write a Python function that turns a list of (key, value) pairs into a dictionary mapping each key to the list of all its values in order (a multimap). Handles an empty list, repeated keys, and None values.

title: dict-from-lists-with-fill
text: Write a Python function that builds a dictionary from a keys list and a values list where extra keys get a fill value (default None) and extra values are ignored. Handles empty lists and a completely empty values list.

title: first-non-repeated-character
text: Write a Python function that returns the first character of a string that appears exactly once, counting case-insensitively but returning the character in its original case. Returns None for the empty string or when every character repeats.

title: dict-common-keys-sum
text: Write a Python function that takes two dictionaries of numbers and returns a dictionary of only the keys present in both, with the two values summed. Handles empty dictionaries, no overlap, and negative values.
code:
def common_keys_sum(d1, d2):
    return {k: d1[k] + d2[k] for k in d1 if k in d2}

title: seconds-to-hh-mm-ss
text: Write a Python function that converts a number of seconds into a zero-padded 'HH:MM:SS' string. Handles zero, values under a minute, and the last second of a day.

title: debug-count-vowels-misses-uppercase
debug-factorial-missing-base-case
debug-sum-to-n-off-by-one-range
text:
This function should sum the integers from 1 to n inclusive but has an off-by-one bug -- range(1, n) excludes n itself:
def sum_to_n(n):
    total = 0
    for i in range(1, n):
        total += i
    return total
Find and fix the bug; it must also return 0 for n = 0.

title: debug-mutable-default-argument
text:
This function should append an item to a list, creating a new list when none is given, but has the classic mutable-default-argument bug -- the default list is shared across calls, so results accumulate:
def append_item(item, items=[]):
    items.append(item)
    return items
Find and fix the bug so each call without a list gets a fresh empty list.

title: debug-average-integer-division
debug-is-vs-equals-string-compare
text:
This function should check whether the user answered yes, but it has a bug -- it compares strings with 'is' (identity) instead of '==' (equality), so it fails unpredictably:
def is_yes(answer):
    return answer.strip().lower() is "yes"
Find and fix the bug; it must handle surrounding whitespace, any letter case, and the empty string.
code:
def is_yes(answer):
    return answer.strip().lower() == "yes"

title: debug-missing-return-statement
text:
This function should return a new list with every number doubled, but it has a bug -- it builds the result and never returns it, so callers get None:
def double_all(nums):
    result = [x * 2 for x in nums]
Find and fix the bug; it must handle the empty list and negative numbers.

title: debug-loop-variable-shadowing
debug-and-vs-or-condition
text:
This function should report whether x lies outside the inclusive range [low, high], but it has a bug -- it uses 'and' where it needs 'or', so the condition can never be true:
def is_out_of_range(x, low, high):
    return x < low and x > high
Find and fix the bug; boundary values low and high count as inside the range.
code:
def is_out_of_range(x, low, high):
    return x < low or x > high

title: debug-find-max-reversed-comparison
debug-word-count-overwrites-instead-of-increment
debug-binary-search-off-by-one-bounds
debug-fibonacci-wrong-base-case
debug-palindrome-index-off-by-one
debug-reverse-string-drops-first-char
text:
This function should reverse a string by walking it backwards, but it has an off-by-one bug -- range(len(s) - 1, 0, -1) stops before index 0, so the first character is dropped:
def reverse_string(s):
    result = ""
    for i in range(len(s) - 1, 0, -1):
        result += s[i]
    return result
Find and fix the bug; it must handle the empty string and a single character.
